QF.id: Registration, Code of Conduct and General Obligations_28

question.text: Which of the following is specifically identified as a core merchant banking activity that CANNOT be outsourced under Regulation 8(i)?

answer.text: Printing and dispatch of offer documents
Due diligence and preparation of offer-related documents
Advertising and marketing of the issue

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Regulation 8(i) explicitly prohibits outsourcing of two core activities: (i) due diligence and (ii) preparation of offer-related documents. These are fundamental to the MB's accountability. **The Trap:** Printing/dispatch and collection of applications are operational activities that can be outsourced, making them tempting distractors for candidates who confuse operational tasks with core MB functions. **Cross-Exam Context:** The concept of non-outsourceable core activities is also relevant in NISM Series V-A (Mutual Fund) regarding AMC responsibilities. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Due diligence is the MB's personal stamp of credibility — outsourcing it would defeat the very purpose of SEBI registration.
